name: bespunky-voice
description: Hear Claude's questions and answer by voice — for when you're away from the screen. SPEAK: when Claude asks something, get a listenable, audio-dedicated summary (no markup or structure read aloud, just the gist and the options), on demand with `/voice say` or automatically with `/voice auto on` (a PreToolUse hook then speaks every multiple-choice question and plan-approval as it appears; default off; free-text questions via `/voice say`). ANSWER: `/voice answer` captures your spoken reply, transcribes it locally, and hands it back as your answer — no typing. TTS sits behind a swappable boundary (a free espeak-ng floor on Linux and `say` on macOS, auto-upgraded to a local Piper neural voice via scripts/install-piper.sh, built to allow a cloud voice). STT is local whisper.cpp, installed opt-in with scripts/install-whisper.sh (base.en floor → small.en for accuracy); listen.sh boosts the mic itself to compensate for WSLg's low input gain. Needs a synthesis engine, a player (paplay/aplay/afplay), Node, a reachable audio sink (WSLg's PULSE_SERVER in the BeSpunky devcontainer) and — to answer — a microphone plus whisper. Both halves are manual/opt-in today; a hands-free mode (Claude asks aloud and waits for your spoken answer, via an MCP tool) is the planned next tier.
